human_verification.notes: Reviewed validated readiness for planning-phase behavior, CPG-specific traceability and quality boundaries, functionality coverage, and runtime-specification-based posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest enforces recommendation-only planning boundaries for demand, schedule, and allocation decisions with explicit routing to quality, production planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, logistics, and approver lanes.
Authoritative sources cover planning workflow, food-safety and records boundaries, traceability expectations, and nonconformance or release handling rather than only one dimension.
Functionality map covers all required shared, planning-scheduling-and-allocation, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities with scenario coverage for every scenario in evaluation results.
Research summary maps workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, and required scenario families to this CPG demand-planning lane.

review_notes: No functional or evidence blockers remain for this package draft.
All required scenario families and escalation families are represented, including traceability conflict, quality hold, supplier substitution, final approval refusal, and tenant adaptation.

notes: validated conditions met for recommendation-only CPG demand-planning specialist.
Runtime remains specification-based and must be promoted to a retained implementation only through separate governance and commit history.
